source.title: Temu Business Model Evolution: Fully Managed to Semi-Managed Transition

extractions.claim: Temu operates two distinct business models with fundamentally different unit economics
extractions.evidence: Fully managed: merchant supplies product to Temu warehouse at supply price, Temu sets retail price, handles sales/marketing/logistics/after-sales. Temu keeps the spread (estimated 35-40% of retail price). Semi-managed (launched March 2024): merchant ships to consumer country via sea freight, stores in local warehouses, delivers to buyer. Temu handles traffic, promotion, pricing, after-sales. Commission estimated at low double-digits of GMV.

verification.verifier_notes: Model descriptions confirmed across multiple sources including TechBuzz China, ChinaTalk, and merchant guides.

extractions.claim: US tariff elimination of de minimis exemption in mid-2025 caused Temu to halve fully-managed US supply, shift 30% to semi-managed
extractions.evidence: Temu temporarily halted fully managed business (China-to-US direct shipping) when 120% tariffs applied. Restarted when tariffs dropped to 54% on May 12, 2025. Result: fully managed supply halved in Q2 2025, 30% converted to semi-managed, total merchandise volume fell 20-30%. Temu suspended US marketing investment on May 1, 2025.

verification.verifier_notes: Confirmed by TechBuzz China Temu Watch series and multiple industry sources.

extractions.claim: Semi-managed now represents ~34% of Temu's global transaction volume, expected to grow to primary model
extractions.evidence: As of mid-2025, semi-managed accounts for 34% of global Temu transaction volume. Semi-managed expected to contribute >$10B in revenue. Self-operated US warehouses projected to handle 15-20% of US volume in 2025, 20-25% by 2026. EU becoming ~40% of Temu's GMV as US becomes more hostile.

verification.verifier_notes: Industry analyst estimates. Temu does not disclose official segment splits.
